profile.name: Elena Duarte
profile.headline: The Business Traveller · books stays for work trips
profile.location: Travels UK & EU · based in Madrid
profile.bio: Elena is 44, a regional sales director who is on the road two weeks a month. She books a place to stay the way she books a flight: fast, on her phone, between meetings. What she wants from a stay is zero friction, a clear listing, a check-in that just works when she lands late, and everything she needs in one place so she is not chasing a host for the wifi at 11pm. Draft persona, sharpen with persona-refine.

sections.title: Jobs to be done
content: Book a reliable place to stay quickly, without reading twenty reviews to feel safe.
Arrive late and get in without a phone call or a scavenger hunt for the key.
Have everything the stay needs, access, wifi, directions, in one place, not scattered across messages.

sections.title: Pains
content: Uncertainty: photos and reality don't always match, and a bad stay derails a work trip.
Access hassle: unclear check-in and chasing the host for codes when she lands late.
Fragmented info: the address is in one message, the wifi in another, the checkout time nowhere.

sections.title: Lifestyle
sections.content: Elena lives out of a cabin bag and treats a stay as infrastructure for the trip, not a holiday. She has no patience for friction, the stay should recede into the background so she can do the work she came to do.
